title: Neolaugeria apiculata
taxonomy: Neolaugeria apiculata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. This family includes a wide range of plants commonly known as heath or heather. Neolaugeria apiculata is a member of the order Ericales and the class Magnoliopsida. The class Magnoliopsida is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many other species of heaths and related plants. Neolaugeria apiculata is also a type of shrub native to the southeastern United States.
uses: Neolaugeria apiculata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties in various cultures. The plant is believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Additionally, it has been used to treat digestive issues and as a natural remedy for fever. The plant's leaves are also used to make teas that are said to promote relaxation and reduce stress. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Neolaugeria apiculata for medicinal purposes.

parts: Neolaugeria apiculata, also known as Key Rosemary or Key Broom, is a plant species native to the Florida Keys. The medicinal parts of Neolaugeria apiculata are typically the leaves, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The leaves of this plant are rich in essential oils, including borneol, camphor, and limonene, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
